faculty: Faculty of Civil and Industrial Engineering
admission_test_type: English TOLC-I (CISIA, also TOLC@HOME mode) or SAT General Test (College Board)
applicant_route: non-EU residing in Italy with valid residence permit (EU-equivalent)
non-EU residing abroad (visa-required, MoveIN pre-selection route)
application_platform: Infostud (Sapienza), CISIA/College Board (test), QS MoveIN (non-EU pre-selection), Universitaly (visa pre-enrolment)
access_quota_non_eu: Not stated as a fixed numerus-clausus quota in the call; admission is by passing the English TOLC-I/SAT (converted score above zero) rather than a ranked place quota. [uncertain]
places_and_foreign_contingent: The call does not publish a fixed number of total or non-EU places; access is test-based (threshold), not numerus clausus. [uncertain]
academic_requirements: Upper secondary school diploma (obtained or to be obtained in the application year). Candidates with a non-Italian secondary qualification must follow the document requirements in Annex 2 (section A2.5) of the call. Admission requires passing the English TOLC-I (or SAT) and meeting the B2 English proficiency requirement. Disability/certified invalidity candidates are exempt from the test.
country_specific_eligibility: Country-specific qualification, minimum schooling years and document/validation requirements are governed by the MoveIN academic requirements PDF for non-EU applicants (academic_requirements_sapienza_2026_2027). [uncertain]
curricular_integration_policy: Further Required Courses (OFAs) may be assigned by the Faculty (see section 9 of the call); for disability/invalidity candidates OFAs are verified and assigned by the Faculty.
conditional_enrolment_policy: Applicants may hold a diploma obtained or to be obtained in the application year; non-EU visa applicants must complete Universitaly pre-enrolment by the stated deadline.
language_requirements: English proficiency of at least B2 (CEFR) is required for admission. Proven by one of: IELTS minimum 5.5; TOEFL iBT minimum 80; Cambridge English B2 (First Certificate or higher); Trinity College London ISE (level 2 or higher); OR by being an English native speaker, holding a diploma/degree taught in English from an accredited EU/EEA/Schengen/Australia/Canada/New Zealand/UK/US institution, holding an IB Diploma or GCE/equivalent, or a diploma/degree where English is the main language of instruction.
language_submission_timing: English B2 proficiency must be met for admission/enrolment; submitted as part of the admission/enrolment procedure. [uncertain]
italian_language_requirement: Not required for this English-taught programme (Italian language test applies only to non-EU applicants choosing Italian-language curricula). Recommended for daily life. [uncertain]
application_deadline_eu: For 2026-2027, EU and EU-equivalent candidates can apply until 31 July 2026 (central international admissions deadline). Programme-specific 2025-2026 call required the test and €10 fee by 30 October 2025 and first tuition instalment by 6 November 2025. [uncertain]
application_deadline_non_eu: For 2026-2027, non-EU visa applicants must complete MoveIN pre-selection by 15 May 2026 (central deadline). In the 2025-2026 call the non-EU MoveIN deadline was 29 April 2025 and Universitaly pre-enrolment by 30 June 2025. [uncertain]
application_fee: €30 MoveIN pre-selection fee per application (non-EU route), plus a separate €10 Infostud fee required to record the TOLC result and enrol.
visa_preenrolment_deadline: Universitaly pre-enrolment for the study visa; in the 2025-2026 call the deadline was 30 June 2025. For 2026-2027 follow the central admissions calendar. [uncertain]
academic_year: 2026-2027 (central international rules); programme-specific call data from 2025-2026 (Rector's Decree no. 1683/2025) [uncertain]
required_documents: For non-Italian qualifications: documents per call Annex 2 (A2.5) and the Sapienza enrolment-for-foreign-qualification page (typically Declaration of Value / CIMEA statement, legalized/apostilled and translated diploma and transcript)
English language certificate or proof of exemption (B2)
English TOLC-I or SAT score report (SAT report sent to segrstudenti.ingegneria@uniroma1.it)
For non-EU visa applicants: pre-acceptance letter from MoveIN and Universitaly pre-enrolment
document_translation_authentication: Foreign secondary qualifications generally require legalization/apostille, official translation into Italian, and a Declaration of Value (DoV) or CIMEA statement of comparability, per the Sapienza enrolment page for non-Italian qualifications (call Annex 2 A2.5). [uncertain]
entry_exam_or_test: English TOLC-I (CISIA online, also TOLC@HOME mode) OR SAT General Test (College Board). The €10 Infostud fee must be paid for the TOLC result to be recorded.
test_variant_and_session: English TOLC-I per CISIA schedule; in the 2025-2026 call the test window was 1 July 2024 to 30 October 2025 (TOLC) and SAT from 1 January 2023 to 30 October 2025. Sessions vary by CISIA calendar. [uncertain]
test_score_conversion_or_threshold: A converted total score above zero is required to pass; the call includes a SAT-to-English-TOLC-I concordance table (Annex 1, A1.6). Below-threshold candidates may be assigned Further Required Courses (OFAs). [uncertain]

source_quotes.quote: Call for applications for admission to the English language bachelor's degree in Sustainable Building Engineering (Rieti campus). Class: L-23
take the English TOLC-I also in TOLC@HOME mode or the SAT; register for pre-selection (compulsory only if you have non-EU citizenship and reside abroad)

English language proficiency of at least level B2 according to the CEFR is required for admission... IELTS with a minimum score of 5.5; TOEFL iBT with minimum score 80; Cambridge English B2 (First certificate or higher); Trinity College London ISE (level 2 or higher)
Register for the pre-selection by entering your application on the QS MoveIn platform... A €30 payment is required, which is separate from the subsequent €10 fee.

The deadline for visa seeking candidates applying to programs on the MoveIN pre-selection platform is May 15, 2026, and all other candidates will be able to submit a pre-selection application until July 31, 2026.

uncertainty_notes: Programme-specific 2026-2027 Call for Applications not yet published at research time; admission mechanics drawn from the 2025-2026 call (Rector's Decree 1683/2025). Deadlines and exact test windows will shift for 2026-2027.

Non-EU deadline 15 May 2026 and EU deadline 31 July 2026 are the central international-admissions dates; the programme's own test/enrolment deadlines (2025-2026: 30 October 2025 test, 6 November 2025 enrolment) differ and a 2026-2027 equivalent was not available.
No fixed non-EU places quota is published in the call; access is by passing the English TOLC-I/SAT threshold rather than a ranked numerus-clausus quota.

Country-specific eligibility, intake semester, language submission timing and document authentication inferred from the MoveIN academic requirements PDF and general Sapienza enrolment pages, not from a programme-specific 2026-2027 source.
official_program_url confirmed via English-filtered catalogue search (course/33482).
